task: Implemented task queue system for multi-developer workflows, fixed timestamp edge case in team display, and committed Stage 3 & 4 features to main branch
discussion_summary: Session focused on integrating a persistent task queue into the session lifecycle so teammates can queue work across sessions. Injected queue into session_start.py main(), created askr_state/tasks directory structure, tested task list/queue/drain commands end-to-end, and fixed a negative-timestamp display bug in the team command that showed future handover times. All Stage 3 (task queue) and Stage 4 (team display) work was committed and pushed.

accomplishments.what: Injected task queue into session_start.py main() to drain queued tasks at session start
Created askr_state/tasks directory and initialized queue file for bippin user
Tested task list, task queue, and task drain commands end-to-end via CLI
Fixed timestamp edge case in askr team display (negative time → 'active now')
Committed Stage 3 (task queue) and Stage 4 (team display) to git and pushed

next_actions.action: Verify git push completed successfully by checking remote branch matches local HEAD
next_actions.why: Last bash command was git push but output was truncated; confirm Stage 3 & 4 are live on remote

next_actions.action: Update askr_state/implementation_state.md to mark Stage 3 and Stage 4 as Complete and commit
next_actions.why: implementation_state.md is still uncommitted and needs to reflect that task queue and team features are now done

next_actions.action: Test multi-user scenario: queue a task for another user and verify it appears in their task list
next_actions.why: Single-user testing is complete; need to validate cross-user queue isolation and retrieval

next_actions.action: Review Stage 5 goals (daemon monitoring, context triggers, auto-launch) and plan implementation
next_actions.why: Stages 3 & 4 are shipped; next phase requires daemon enhancements and context-aware session triggering

decisions.decision: Task queue stored as flat markdown file in askr_state/tasks/ per-user, not in database
decisions.reason: Keeps state conflict-free and git-friendly; simple append/drain model avoids locking

decisions.decision: Timestamp edge case (future handover time) handled by displaying 'active now' instead of negative duration
decisions.reason: Cleaner UX than showing -5m; handles clock skew gracefully without breaking team display

relational_files.why: Contains cmd_task and cmd_team entry points that call queue/drain/list logic
Called at session start; now drains task queue before running user commands
Tracks completion status of Stages 1–5; needs update to mark Stage 3 & 4 done
